title: Layer Normalization
concept: Layer Normalization stabilizes training by scaling activations across features per token (distinct from BatchNorm's batch scaling, callback to Unit 110).

exercises.prompt: What is the main goal of using normalization techniques like Layer Normalization?

options.text: Stabilize and scale internal network activations
Compress the final model file size
Speed up the user's internet connection

exercises.explain: Layer Normalization is used to keep the internal values (activations) of a neural network stable, preventing them from getting too large or too small during training.

cards.title: Keep Your Numbers Balanced!
cards.body: When training deep neural networks, numbers can sometimes blow up to be giant or shrink to near zero, causing the AI to get confused. How do we keep these numbers in a healthy, stable range without making training slow and complicated? Meet Layer Normalization!

cards.title: What is Layer Normalization?
cards.body: Layer Normalization (LayerNorm) is a technique that scales and shifts the values (activations) inside a neural network. Instead of looking at other data samples in a batch, it calculates the average and variance across all features for a single data point. This keeps the values stable and prevents training from slowing down or crashing.

cards.title: The Custom Tailor
cards.body:
Imagine a classroom where students of all heights stand in a line. 

Instead of comparing Student A to other students in different classrooms (Batch Norm), Layer Normalization is like adjusting the scale of each student's outfit based *only* on their own measurements. Each student is balanced individually, regardless of who else is in the classroom!

cards.title: LayerNorm in Action
cards.body:
In a language model processing the word "robot", the word is represented by a list of numbers (features) like `[12.0, -0.5, 3.5, 25.0]`. 

LayerNorm takes this specific list, calculates its average and spread, and rescales these four numbers to be closer to a standard range (like having an average of 0). It does this for "robot" without needing to look at any other words in the sentence!

cards.title: The Norm Confusion
cards.body:
**Myth:** Layer Normalization and Batch Normalization are the exact same thing.
**Fact:** They are different! Batch Normalization scales numbers across different training examples, which can fail if your examples (like sentences) are different lengths. Layer Normalization scales numbers *within* a single example, making it perfect for text and sequences.

cards.title: Batch vs. Layer
cards.body: Let's compare Batch Normalization (which you met in Unit 110) with Layer Normalization to see how they look at data differently.

right.text: Looks only within a single sample's features to find the average.

left.text: Looks across multiple different samples in a batch to find the average.

exercises.prompt: Why is Layer Normalization preferred over Batch Normalization in Natural Language Processing (NLP)?

options.text: It requires massive batch sizes to work properly.
It works on each token individually, making it perfect for varying sentence lengths.
It completely removes the need for activation functions.
It only works on image datasets.

exercises.explain: Layer Normalization is highly favored for text because it normalizes each word (token) individually, making it independent of batch size or varying sentence lengths.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Layer Normalization can easily process a batch containing only a single sentence.
exercises.explain: Unlike Batch Normalization, Layer Normalization works on individual data points, meaning its behavior does not change based on how many samples are in a batch.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to complete how Layer Normalization operates.
exercises.sentence: Layer Normalization scales activations across all _____ within a single token.
exercises.explain: Layer Normalization operates on a single token at a time, calculating the average and variance across all of its features.

exercises.prompt: Which AI assistant correctly describes how Layer Normalization calculates its averages?

options.text: AI Model A: "Layer Normalization calculates the average value across all samples in the current batch."
AI Model B: "Layer Normalization calculates the average value across all features of a single data point."
AI Model C: "Layer Normalization scales values using the historical average of the entire dataset."

exercises.explain: AI Model B is correct because Layer Normalization calculates the statistics (mean and variance) across the features of a single sequence step (or token) at a time, not across the batch dimension.

exercises.prompt: You are building a custom Transformer model. How does Layer Normalization process the activations of your tokens?

options.text: It normalizes values across the entire batch of different sentences.
It normalizes the feature values within each individual token independently.
It scales values using the average of previous training steps.
It only normalizes the final output layer of the network.

exercises.explain: Layer Normalization looks at each individual token (or data point) and balances all of its feature values internally. It does not look across different examples in the batch, which makes it perfect for sequences of varying lengths, like sentences!
